topic: How to structure your resume for AI ATS filters
key_points: ATS scanning algorithms don't read PDFs well; stick to simple DOCX files.
Match your resume keywords exactly to the job description.
Use clean, single-column layouts. Avoid complex grids, text boxes, and charts.
Quantify your accomplishments. Say 'increased signups by 45%' instead of 'helped with marketing'.

topic: 3 cold emailing templates that got internships at top tech startups
key_points: Subject line must be specific and value-driven (e.g., 'A quick observation about [Company] design').
Keep the email under 150 words. Be concise and straight to the point.
Show, don't tell: share a 1-page notion document of custom ideas you made for them.
The call to action should be low-friction: 'Open to a quick 10m chat next Tuesday?'

topic: The hidden networking loophole: LinkedIn Alumni Tool
key_points: Go to your university's LinkedIn page and click 'Alumni'.
Filter by the city you want to work in and the target company.
Send a warm message asking for advice, not a job: 'How did you navigate the transition from [Univ] to [Company]?'
80% of jobs are never publicly advertised; they are filled via warm referrals.

topic: Why JobInGen is banning unpaid internships
key_points: Unpaid internships are elitist; only students who can afford to work for free can take them.
Our mission is democratizing career opportunities for all students, regardless of background.
Every employer partnering with JobInGen must commit to paying at least local minimum wage.
We believe talent is evenly distributed, but opportunity is not.

topic: Meet your new AI career co-pilot
key_points: JobInGen isn't just a job board. It's an intelligent engine designed to guide you step-by-step.
From resume feedback to matching you with vetted tech startups, our AI guides your job search.
Sign up today and get your personalized career roadmaps in under 5 minutes.

topic: The 'hustle culture' myth: Why resting is actually productive
key_points: Working 80-hour weeks causes burnout, which destroys long-term cognitive output.
At JobInGen, we practice focused 4-hour deep work blocks rather than 12-hour low-focus grinds.
Take your weekends off. Go for a run, read a book, sleep. Great code and ideas come from a rested brain.

topic: Behind the scenes: The JobInGen team's favorite slack channels
key_points: #cringe-corporate-speak: Where we post the worst corporate jargon we find.
#pets-of-jobingen: Showing off our remote working assistants (cats and dogs).
#failed-ideas: Celebrating things we tried that flopped, because that is how we learn.
